purpose: Automation guidance for AccessiView regression, accessibility, and live-site exploratory testing.
safetyRules: Prefer local fixtures for blocking regression checks.
Use public pages that do not require login for live exploratory checks.
Do not submit forms, create accounts, purchase items, post content, or change user data during automation.
Do not collect personal data, cookies, session tokens, page text dumps, or screenshots containing private information.
Treat live-site results as advisory because website layouts and anti-automation behavior can change at any time.
Keep browser extension permissions and content security policy unchanged unless a reviewed feature requires it.

steps: Load manifest.json.
Verify Manifest V3 is used.
Verify the expected service worker and content script ordering.
Verify permissions remain in the reviewed allowlist.
Verify extension CSP does not allow unsafe inline scripts or eval.
Enable Simplified Page mode.
Open an article-style page.
Verify the main article region is preserved.
Verify navigation, sidebars, comments, and repeated clutter are reduced without hiding the main content.
Enable Focus mode with the text-only reader overlay.
Open a content-heavy page.
Verify the overlay is readable and keyboard reachable.
Verify useful inline links remain visible, clickable, and keyboard focusable.
Verify the exit control is visible, named, and clickable.
Enable high contrast mode.
Enable reading text adjustments.
Verify text, links, form controls, and page surfaces remain visible.
Verify images are not inverted unless the setting is enabled.
Enable Reduce Motion mode.
Open a page with smooth scrolling, animation, or autoplay media.
Verify animations and transitions are reduced.
Verify smooth scrolling is converted to instant scrolling.
Verify autoplay media is paused when browser policies allow it.
Enable the reading guide.
Move pointer and keyboard focus through the page.
Verify the guide follows focus when configured.
Verify guide movement does not steal focus or block controls.
Enable Navigation Helpers.
Tab through links, buttons, inputs, and custom controls.
Verify focus rings are visible.
Verify keyboard map can open and close.
Verify larger target mode does not break layout.
Enable missing-alt warnings.
Open a page with informative images.
Verify images without alt text are outlined.
Verify decorative images with empty alt text are not flagged.
Enable Form Helper mode.
Open a fixture with required, invalid, and unlabeled controls.
Verify required fields, invalid fields, and unlabeled controls are marked.
Verify no form is submitted during automation.
Enable Cognitive Support mode.
Open a long-form page.
Verify headings are emphasized.
Verify long paragraphs are chunked.
Verify main content remains readable.
Request a local summary for a content page.
Use the offline extractive engine in CI-style automation.
Verify the summary returns a success response.
Verify sensitive-field pages are not summarized.
Start read aloud on detected page text.
Verify pause, resume, previous, next, and stop controls respond.
Verify current-speech highlighting can be enabled.
Avoid asserting a specific installed voice because browser voices vary by machine.
Enable separate settings for the current website.
Use the manual main-content picker on an article region.
Reload the page.
Verify the saved selector is applied before generic reader detection.
Clear the pick and verify the site override is removed.
Open a fixture with shadow DOM and iframe content.
Enable contrast, motion, navigation, form, and alt-warning modes.
Verify supported open shadow roots and accessible frames receive styles or markers.
Record browser-restricted frames as limitations instead of failures.
Open popup.html and options.html.
Verify presets, undo controls, profile controls, summary controls, and side-panel controls exist.
Verify status text uses live-region semantics.
Verify controls can be reached by keyboard.

websiteCategories.name: News and article sites
websiteCategories.whyTest: Article extraction, clutter reduction, sticky regions, ads, headings, summaries, and reading modes.

websiteCategories.name: Video, audio, and rich media sites
websiteCategories.whyTest: Reduce motion, autoplay handling, captions, media controls, overlays, focus order, and image-heavy layouts.

websiteCategories.name: Shopping, marketplace, and booking sites
websiteCategories.whyTest: Dense cards, filters, product images, forms, dialogs, ratings, target size, and keyboard navigation.

websiteCategories.name: Social, community, and forum sites
websiteCategories.whyTest: Infinite feeds, dialogs, comments, nested content, focus handling, dynamic updates, and visual overload.

websiteCategories.name: Documentation, learning, and reference sites
websiteCategories.whyTest: Long structured text, tables, code blocks, sidebars, headings, search, and summary quality.

websiteCategories.name: Search, maps, productivity, and app-style pages
websiteCategories.whyTest: Complex app shells, forms, toolbars, keyboard focus, dialogs, canvas-like surfaces, and dynamic updates.

websiteCatalog.name: Wikipedia
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Long articles, headings, tables, links, images, language switching, and stable public content.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Prefer article pages over the home page.

websiteCatalog.name: YouTube
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Media-heavy layout, dynamic content, keyboard focus, autoplay behavior, captions, and motion safety.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Do not sign in or interact with recommendations beyond navigation checks.

websiteCatalog.name: Google Search
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Dense links, search controls, dynamic result regions, keyboard navigation, and focus visibility.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Use a harmless query and avoid account-specific features.

websiteCatalog.name: Bing Search
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Search results with cards, side regions, images, and dynamic layout changes.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Use public search result pages only.

websiteCatalog.name: Amazon
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Complex navigation, product cards, forms, ratings, images, and sticky regions.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Do not sign in, add items to cart, or purchase.

websiteCatalog.name: eBay
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Search forms, product listings, filters, image grids, and repeated controls.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Do not bid, buy, sign in, or save listings.

websiteCatalog.name: Printables
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Svelte marketplace homepage with dense model cards, carousels, badges, and mixed dark media surfaces that can expose Simplified Page color inheritance issues.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Use public pages only; do not sign in, download, like, collect, or upload models.

websiteCatalog.name: Reddit
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Infinite feeds, nested comments, dynamic regions, overlays, and keyboard focus behavior.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Use public pages and do not post, vote, or log in.

websiteCatalog.name: X
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Highly dynamic feed UI, dialogs, keyboard navigation, images, and motion-heavy interactions.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Many flows require login; keep checks exploratory and non-mutating.

websiteCatalog.name: Facebook
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Complex app shell, dialogs, forms, and dynamic content regions.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Do not sign in or interact with private content.

websiteCatalog.name: Instagram
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Image-first layouts, dialogs, focus handling, and login-gated public content.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Do not sign in or interact with accounts.

websiteCatalog.name: LinkedIn
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Forms, dialogs, navigation, job listings, and dense professional content.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Avoid authenticated flows and profile interactions.

websiteCatalog.name: GitHub
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Code views, issue forms, markdown content, navigation landmarks, and tables.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Use public repositories and do not submit issues or comments.

websiteCatalog.name: Stack Overflow
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Question pages, code blocks, voting controls, forms, and dense text.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Use public question pages and do not vote or post.

websiteCatalog.name: Medium
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Article pages, overlays, sticky headers, and long-form reading.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Prefer public articles; paywalls or sign-in prompts may appear.

websiteCatalog.name: The New York Times
websiteCatalog.whyTest: News article layouts, ads, paywall prompts, images, and sticky navigation.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Treat paywall and consent overlays as site conditions, not extension failures.

websiteCatalog.name: BBC
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Article pages, navigation regions, media embeds, captions, and international content.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Use public news pages.

websiteCatalog.name: CNN
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Dynamic article pages, media, ads, sticky regions, and related-content blocks.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Consent prompts and autoplay behavior may vary by region.

websiteCatalog.name: MSN
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Built-in AccessiView rule pack, portal layout, article cards, ads, and recommendations.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Useful for regression checks around existing site rules.

websiteCatalog.name: Thaiware
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Built-in AccessiView rule pack, Thai language content, article layouts, and ads.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Useful for bilingual and regional layout checks.

websiteCatalog.name: Gmail
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Complex app shell, keyboard shortcuts, forms, dialogs, and high interaction density.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Do not use private accounts in automation; prefer a dedicated test account only if approved.

websiteCatalog.name: Google Docs
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Canvas-like editing surface, toolbars, dialogs, focus behavior, and keyboard shortcuts.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Requires a controlled test account and test document for meaningful automation.

websiteCatalog.name: Google Maps
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Canvas/map content, panels, search forms, dynamic controls, and keyboard navigation.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Map canvas limitations should be recorded as known constraints.

websiteCatalog.name: Netflix
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Media-heavy UI, motion, dialogs, keyboard focus, and protected video surfaces.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Authenticated DRM/video playback should be manual or use a dedicated test account only.

websiteCatalog.name: Microsoft Learn
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Long technical documents, navigation trees, code blocks, and headings.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Good stable target for public documentation checks.

websiteCatalog.name: MDN Web Docs
websiteCatalog.whyTest: Reference pages, examples, code blocks, sidebars, and article content.
websiteCatalog.automationNotes: Good stable target for documentation-style pages.
